posts.author: TheAnalyzer
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 2

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R2
Damn it’s been a LONG time since I’ve last been on here! Hope everyone’s well. I just had leg surgery like 4 days ago, and dare I say this is a BANGER card to come home to!!!!! I think I’m going to settle with Jiri here as my prediction. I was initially going to place a bet on Ulberg, as he does have a bit of a Pereira-esque style which Jiri seems to struggle with a lot. Heavy low kicks, and timed power shots, somewhat patient striking and etc. But at the end of the day, Ulberg is simply not Pereira. He doesn’t hit nearly as hard, his leg kicks are also not nearly as effective, and he has a lot of flaws which I think can really do him in here. Ulberg can be rather open to getting hit a lot of the time, and historically he does not like being pressured. Jiri’s entire style is to pressure his opponent. Now obviously the Rountree fight was a bit strange as he didn’t do that until round 3, but when he finally did it, Rountree gassed like a **** and Jiri put him out cold. Ulberg can be a good counter striker at times against people who try to pressure him, but their pressure is nothing like Jiri. And Jiri also hits extremely hard. I’d argue he’s now the hardest hitter in the division now that Pereira’s going to 265 which is honestly mental since Jiri is the smallest one here. I also don’t see Ulberg having enough power to chin Jiri as he wasn’t able to finish people who don’t have amazing chins, and I don’t see him fighting perfectly for very long either once he realizes what he’s in for. He may be winning early, but eventually I think the pressure will get to him and Jiri will win by a late second round KO. I think this will be very similar to the Rakic fight for Jiri.

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, DEC
I do not think highly of Murzakanov at all, but this is a rather easy fight for him. The main issues with Murzakanov are that he’s very short for this weight class, has close to no wingspan, and is very much a fluke merchant. That said, I actually think he wins this time without a fluke. I’m still not convinced that Costa takes his career seriously even after his last performance. Now I’ll give him credit where it’s due, it was a good performance. But that was against a very mid kickboxer who apparently forgot how to kickbox that night. Also despite being younger than Azamat in actual age (which is just insane), he’s definitely older in fight years. The injuries and infections throughout his career have definitely caught up to him over the past almost decade now. In fact, the last time Costa looked good before his last fight was all the way back in 2019 against a past-prime Yoel Romero. And you can argue he lost that fight too. Since then Izzy ****** him both doggy style and anally, he managed to get pieced up in moments by VETTORI, and couldn’t even chin Luke Rockhold who was WELL past his peak. And then he did have one good moment against Whittaker, but then got right back to getting pieced up. And then he looked scared ****less against Strickland (still baffles me how he was one scorecard away from winning due to that one crackhead judge (Dave Tirelli). So after all of that, I simply cannot trust Costa here. I am very much anti-Azamat but there’s nothing here that shows me he doesn’t win this fight. However I will say he wins by decision this time as Costa still actually has a great chin. And most of the people Azamat are finishing are not known for having the greatest chins ever. I can also see Costa potentially having a late rally even as he does have better cardio than Azamat as well. But I’m going to take the more serious, undefeated fighter who also just seems better at this point. Azamat 29-28.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, TKO, R1
If Hokit doesn’t get a flash KO he’s going to get pounded. My main worry for Blaydes is of course his chin, but also that he doesn’t wrestle as much as he used to. However I honestly do trust him to wrestle here. He actually wrestled the entire first round against Kuniev and pretty much dominated the round with it. Even Almeida(so happy he got cut) couldn’t do that. And while I do think Blaydes lost the rest of the fight with Kuniev, I also think Kuniev is quite solid. Maybe not elite, but good enough to hang with most of the division. Hokit on the other hand is honestly just a speed and power merchant. He swings and just manages to connect a kazillion power bombs on the chins of tomato cans. He does have a wrestling background himself and won his first few fights actually by submission, but I don’t think his wrestling is on the level of Blaydes. Blaydes also has Hokit cleared in almost every aspect of striking too except speed. If Hokit does connect one though he is probably going to sleep Blaydes. But if he can’t do that he has no other path to victory. Maybe a bet on Hokit by KO isn’t a bad idea, but there’s really only a 10% chance he wins this fight. I get he’s very marketable and the UFC want to push him, but unless Hokit can fluke it, he is not winning this fight.

Reyes vs. Walker
Dominick Reyes, TKO, R1
Flip a coin really. Both have the power of a hydrogen bomb, and the chin of a coughing baby. I’ll trust Reyes as he’s the more tactical striker in what will be a standup fight.

Swanson vs. Landwehr
Cub Swanson, TKO, R3
Winnable fight for Swanson but also entertaining as **** for his sendoff. I think Swanson chins the train late with a counter shot during a heated exchange. Happy Retirement Cub!

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R2
I still think Pico is very good, Pitbull is just washed at this point.

Holland vs. Brown
Kevin Holland, TKO, R2
Do not bet

Gamrot vs. Ribovics
Mateusz Gamrot, DEC
Ribovics arguably lost to Zellhuber. Not picking him against anybody in the top 100.

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
Suarez seems to be on the decline but she should still win. Godinez’s defensive grappling isn’t great at all and I think Suarez will be able to control her. And maybe even get the guillotine.

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, TKO, R2
I think this will just be a classic Chris Padilla fight. I don’t think either of these guys are necessarily good or bad, but I think Padilla’s finishing potential will make all of the difference. Plus he knows when he’s losing and will show urgency and push a finish when he feels like he needs to. I trust him more here.

Gastelum vs. Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, DEC
Both are washed but seeing Luque fight at this point of his career honestly makes me cry. He was at one point one of the most entertaining fighters on the roster. And then he got a brain bleed from Geoff Neal (who’s also washed now), and just doesn’t have it in him anymore.

Radtke vs. Prado
Charles Radtke, DEC
Radtke 30-27. Doesn’t find a finish this time. Prado the bum gets cut after.

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Holland vs. Brown is really giving me agita. They both have very similar styles, and Holland has made it to greater heights in his career, but his recent loss to D-Rod was... concerning. 2020 Holland would have slept D-Rod badly. At the same time, though, Holland looked okay against Malott considering that he got two horrible nut shots without a point being deducted (Big Dan masterclass). It's possible that Holland has looked mid recently because those back-to-back fights are taking a toll on him in a way that they didn't when he was younger. Holland's taken an actual break to recover since the Malott loss back in October, so maybe he'll come out looking much better than he has recently. But how can I trust that? Randy Brown takes his career way more seriously—his chin is a real sticking point, but I feel like I still have to pick Brown because I will take basically any excuse to not pick Kevin Holland. I don't hate the guy, but I hate picking the guy, if that makes sense.

posts.author: grayerman
Littledohyo
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

I'm having trouble writing up reasons for some of my picks this card. All the fights are pretty even, except luque and Gastelum.

If you take an aerial view of things and look at Gamrot Ribovic, just from outside of UFC and being a fan of the sport, it's clear as day Ribovic wins and it's a change up for the division. I think Gamrot will win and I have always been really high on him, but this matchup seems like it holds one of those sneaky outcomes fans won't see coming at all. I'm not even saying a KO, it's just that a long standing favorite getting smoked by a newcomer that's here to replace him in the rankings looks exactly like this. Can't pick Ribovics though, so I guess I'm getting owned if that happens.

My gut instinct was Cub over Nate because Nate is too willing to take damage and has just been blown the **** out in all of his fights since I started watching. His last win he felt like he was barely scraping by. Yet, somehow Nate decision is calling to me on account of Cub's focus on running a gym. He might just not want to risk getting knocked out, I dunno. I also think that Cub's KO chances are actually a lot lower than people are anticipating, just because he will be slower. The guys Nate fought recently aren't real young, but they are still a lot younger than Cub is now. As the week goes by I'm leaning towards a bloody decision though.

Jiri and Azamat are both similar in that they are coming up for a loss that shocks everyone pretty soon in their careers I think. I do not think Alex is some super hero legendary KO meister with god power (I know this is blasphemy chill guys), I actually do think that Jiri has a cracked chin, which is why he got smoked so bad in those fights. I've been debating on this one a lot too. Thing is, with Azamat, I'd pick most of the division against him, but now Costa just shows up and makes it confusing, but mostly because Costa doesn't fight that much. I do think people picking Azamat by decision are crazy, though.

The Costa that showed up for his last three fights will absolutely out point Azamat handily unless Azamat really cracks him bad, which I guess he can do. I dunno, I get people questioning Costa's heart, and his track record is so weird, but I dislike seeing people say that Rakic was a real test for Azamat in the state he fought in. His performance was terrible - he literally threw away all of his advantages and just gave Azamat the win on a silver platter.

Both guys are Chinny but Reyes has more power. I am going with Reyes

Cub Swanson is 42 and his last win that wasn't against a can was 10 years ago. Give me Nate.... Ok Billy Q isn't necessarily a can but he's not great. Nate actually has surprisingly good wins like Klein & Onama.

Pitbull vs Pico I really don't know what to make of either of these guys. Pitbull is kind of washed but I am not even sure Pico is good. Pitbull is +250 I can probably get him via decision +500 I'll probably sprinkle. Or maybe I'll see what the odds are for (Pico by KO or Pitbull by decision)

Look.... I like Kevin Holland as much as the next guy but when are you guys going to learn? 58% of Kevin Holland is actually insane lol. Randy Brown is going to piece him up.

Gamrot is going to wrestle**** Ribovics to a boring decision right after Suarez does the same to Loopy. These fights are when I will be picking up my to go order from bdubbs lol.

Taco left a bad taste in my mouth after that Jai Herbert performance. I also have bet & picked Marquel in all 3 of his UFC wins. Neither guys are high end by any means but I think Taco is a bit better. Marquel hasn't really shown much. This could definitely be close, the only thing I feel strongly about is this fight is going the distance.

Gastellum vs Luque is kind of tricky. You can't knock out Fat boy but you can sub him and Luque is plenty capable of getting a sub. Both guys haven't really looked good in awhile but Fatboy's win vs Drod is actually aging pretty well. I lean Chubby here if he can make weight. Gastellum looks respectable at times, Luque doesn't really look like he belongs anymore.

Prado sucks gimme Chuck Buffalo

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R3
I did a ton of tape study and went into heavy detail on my picks for last week's card... then I got 6/13 correct. So, I'm going back to low-effort pick-making. This is a heavily vibes-based pick. Ulberg seems like the perfect counter-punchy style to beat a reckless, aggressive fighter like Jiri, but Jiri is a phenomenal athlete and 100% dedicated to winning. Jiri is younger, he's got more dog in him, he will apply the pressure, and he will (I hope) weather an early storm to find the finish. He'll probably get pieced up for R1, dropped, and almost finished, but I just think he's going to get him, man.

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, TKO, R2
Show pictures of these two guys to someone random and ask them who they think is steroid user. The virgin steroid-faker  vs. the chad steroid user. But anyway, I don't see Costa winning here. I've doubted Murzakanov in the past (and even picked Rakic DEC), but he's proved me wrong. Costa's not fighting at his proper weight class, and I think he's going to get thumped.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, DEC
I don't feel confident about this pick, but (A) I want Hokit to lose and (B) I'm skeptical of the hype around him. Clearly, he's very talented, especially by the standards of the current heavyweight roster, but getting a jump up from Denzel Freeman to Curtis Blaydes is insane. Like, couldn't they have given him Shamil? Teixeira? Mick Parkin? Valter Walker? Ryan Spann? Whatever. It's very possible that Hokit runs through Blaydes with aggressive wrestling and more powerful striking. But I'm not gonna fade Blaydes based on a crappy performance against Rizvan Kuniev (who's decent himself) when Blaydes had a torn MCL in R1.

Reyes vs. Walker
Dominick Reyes, TKO, R1
I think Reyes is the better MMA fighter and has the less-glassy chin... I think.

Swanson vs. Landwehr
Cub Swanson, TKO, R2
Might switch on this one. Landwehr was piecing up Morgan Charriere in his last fight until Charriere found the finish, and I think it's possible that Landwehr out-cardios Swanson and finds a finish in R3. However, Cub is the better striker, and he's looked decent for a 42-year-old super-unc. This is a super-low confidence pick and also 100% biased because Cub is Cub, but he's my pick.

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R2
Another low-confidence pick. I thought Ige fought very tentatively against Pitbull and really had no answers for the wrestling. Pico's not going to do those things. Of course, he might run in and get chinned again like he did against Murphy. His chin also might just be plain cooked because he's suffered several bad KOs. But Pico is the much younger guy, and he's got the wrestling needed to not get big-brothered by Pitbull. Odds are stupid, but Pico's still the pick.

Holland vs. Brown
Randy Brown, DEC
Yet another super low-confidence pick. Four in a row. But I will basically look for any reason not to pick Kevin Holland at this point. And while Brown is highly inconsistent and frequently a disappointment, I am at the very least more confident that he is taking his career seriously. I think Bonfim would beat Holland, too.

Gamrot vs. Ribovics
Mateusz Gamrot, DEC
I like Ribovics a lot, but he's going life-or-death with guys like Zellhuber and Elves Brener who are just not ranked material. Maybe that's the same thing I should have said about Chris Duncan, but y'know. Anyway, Gamrot's good. The loss to Hooker was very close, and while Gamrot makes everything super close, I feel confident that he can smudge Ribovics to a 29-27 DEC here.

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
Put that meme of Jonah Hill saying "I guess, bro" here.

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, DEC
I think Mederos is pretty decent despite him making it way too close with Austin Hubbard of all people, but nothing he's done has impressed me as much as Taco's wins. Taco's not going to just stand at range and let Mederos calf kick him to death like Choinski did, either.

Gastelum vs. Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, DEC
Luque has just looked like a shell of himself in there recently. He almost seemed like he didn't want to be there against Buckley and Alvarez. Now he's up a weight class, and while Gastelum is just blobbing around and not much of a threat to any serious contender, I think he's got more left in the tank.

Radtke vs. Prado
Charles Radtke, DEC
I like Prado and I remember having faith in him being able to improve, but damn if he doesn't always find a way to lose and disappoint me. I know that the Veretennikov fight was close, but losing to Veretennikov means you go on the do-not-pick list.

* Edited at 04.08.2026,  2:59 PM ET *

posts.author: Fast_Tulip
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

I'm not sure how the term "lighter man skill" caught on so much. Just cause you were in a lighter weight class, does not mean you have an advantage at a higher weight class. You either have the skill or you don't. If Costa fought Ulberg, he could not lighter man skill him. If you are the more technical boxer or better grappler, you can beat your opponents in those areas unless there's a huge weight advantage. That's why Holloway beat Gaethje, because he just had better boxing in general and nothing to do with his weight. Look at Estevan last week, his lighter man skill did not get him takedowns vs. Ewing cause his skill in general wasn't good enough in grappling

posts.content:
Curtis Blaydes vs. Josh Hokit
End by KO rnd 1

I think Josh is going to go balls to the wall early, I'm just not sure if it works. I liked what Curtis said in interviews; He knows he can't compete and sell him self with his skills so he does his antics instead. I think Curtis wants to put this guy away, his hand speed and accuracy is pretty underrated as well. When two heavyweight wrestlers battle, most of the time the wrestling cancels out in an attempt to fatigue. I'm just not convinced it's gonna work here for Hokit, nor Blaydes and someone gets clipped early. Leaning Blaydes because of the experience but Im also not going to ignore how susceptible he is to power shots that Hokit can find if he makes this a brawl.

Prediction:
Ulberg  |  Decision

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Carlos Ulberg, DEC
Ulberg by 48-47x3 Uni Dec. Amazing fight for the belt. Ulberg's power will keep Jiri honest in certain moments, staggering him and making him pause his gameplan. Ulberg engages strong but disengages before any meaningful counters swing back towards him. The Older Ulberg with a lack of tread compared to the younger Jiri's incredibly seasoned career is a fantastic storyline.

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, TKO, R3
IF Azamat hurts Costa, Two things will go against Costa that have nothing to do with being hurt...Costa urgently tries to escape making things look alot worse then they are in the moment, whereas Azamat swarms and makes his ground and pound look unstoppable and brutal. These two things mixed makes me think we might even see a bad stoppage here, but The ref sees Azamat relentlessly striking whereas Costa is spinning and trying to evade but by the time ref steps in, Costa can't disengage. TKO Victory.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Josh Hokit, TKO, R2
Ground and Pound TKO for The Incredible Hulk. UFC is absolutely desperate for a HW star.

posts.author: sansanich86
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Ulberg looks great on paper - very clean striker, high accuracy, and strong distance control. He spends around 96% of the fight on the feet and lands about 10.3 strikes per round (57% accuracy).
Procházka is the opposite — more chaotic and unpredictable. He spends about 73% of the fight striking and lands 8.8 strikes per round (55% accuracy), but relies more on pressure and wild exchanges.
If Ulberg keeps it technical and disciplined, he probably wins rounds.  But if it gets wild for even a moment, Jiri can capitalize - that’s where he’s most dangerous.
I’m slightly leaning Ulberg, but this feels like a 50/50 fight where anything can happen.
Stats via Stats Fight app: https://statsfight.com/

posts.author: dtomic7
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R3
Ulberg has all the tools to beat Jiri, no doubt. Speed, timing, striking defense and offense, distance management, good anti-grappling, the quintessential CKB style. But will that matter in the octagon with Jiri? You think Jiri cares about that? To beat Jiri you need to have a similar if not better mental than him. We saw that with Poatan, too the point of Jiri accusing him of shamanic magic. It's going to be very typical of Jiri, get his face punched off, lock in, land something, smells blood, and done. Round 3

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, TKO, R2
I actually think Costa could very well win this fight, but I don't want to overcomplicate and overthink these fights. I want to retain my high pick % on Tapology you know.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, DEC
Listen, I'm all for a bit of personality and showmanship, but there has to be an off-switch or just make it less cringe. I was stone-cold straight faced in all his post-fight rambles and pressers. Learn to turn it off. Blaydes by being actually experienced

Reyes vs. Walker
Dominick Reyes, TKO, R1
Battle of the Chins, who ever gets touched dies, the glove touch may even to that. But I think Reyes has been better as of recent.

Swanson vs. Landwehr
Cub Swanson, DEC
Cub rides off into the sunset. The one of last of the early 2000's WEC fighters. (for all those who remember, I was practically a sperm cell)

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R1
Pico is a true glass cannon, but I believe he will have the power and speed to get over an aging Pitbull. Also this would've been Bellator's all-timer super fight, now its just stuck on the prelims of a random numbered event. Interesting.

Holland vs. Brown
Randy Brown, DEC
This fight is really fun but I am kind of over the Kevin Holland "I don't really care and I am doing this for fun" mentality/agenda. Like it was awesome when you fought 6 times in 2020, but now people have expectations for you which you should strive to actually fulfill. Sorry about the little rant lol.

Gamrot vs. Ribovics
Esteban Ribovics, TKO, R3
I am very surprised by the lack of Ribovics truthers on here. Gamrot is susceptible to pressure and cardio, two of the things that make Ribovics so great. Ribovics finishes him late after a competitive fight.

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
Tatiana is unfortunately the victim of having a boring style, I am sure more would like and appreciate her skills if she didn't hug so damn much.

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, SUB, R1
Taco has been great in his 2 year tenure with the UFC, I think I sinches up a slick and tight (pause) choke early.

Gastelum vs. Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, TKO, R2
I think Luque best days are far behind him and Kevin still has that off-switch power at 185. Brutal finish.

Radtke vs. Prado
Charles Radtke, DEC
A very good opener for a very good card, I'm taking Radtke by a dominate decision.

@finncampbell
I hear you. MMA math isn't reliable but we can also look at their two most recent fights for more insight. In the last few years, Holland and Brown have both fought a lot of older guys. But in their most recent fights, they both fought rising prospects. Holland went the distance, Brown got finished (again). Is Mike Mallot as good as Bonfim? No. Are they both younger, rising guys in the division and somewhat comparable challenges? In my opinion. yes.

JDM also isn't the only example of a common opponent where Holland had a drastically better result. Holland smoked Luque but to be fair, Brown fought a much better version of Luque when he was actually decent.

BTW people learn how to use than and then. It’s so frustrating as an educator to see people continuing to mess that up. Sorry just a pet peeve of mine.
Then" refers to time, sequence, or consequence (e.g., "We ate, then left"), while "than" is used for comparisons (e.g., "She is taller than me"). A helpful trick is that "then" relates to time (both have 'e'), and "than" is used to compare (both have 'a').

posts.author: connorbrown16
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Charles Radtke vs. Francisco Prado
Charles Radtke via Decision
Well, this probably isn’t how Prado envisioned his UFC tenure going. He’s young but he’ll be 1-5 in the promotion after this and you’re usually not going to get a 2nd shake after a paltry first go-around. Radtke is basically that bottom rung on the Welterweight totem pole because he can win some fights but he’s mainly here to make the next crop look good. Not saying I’m confident at all ever taking him, but this one he’ll show he wants it more and takes the decision victory.

Kelvin Gastelum vs. Vicente Luque
Kelvin Gastelum via Decision
If this were anyone else against Luque, I’d probably go with a finish but is Gastelum who hasn’t knocked anyone out since 2017. Luque is pretty much shot at this point; injuries and damage have added up and he’s just not what he use to be. I hope this fight doesn’t suck but I have a feeling it will and put people in a grouchy mood so let’s hope for the best as Gastelum outlasts Luque to take the decision.

MarQuel Mederos vs. Chris Padilla
Chris Padilla via 1st Round TKO
Ok, time to restore order to the proceedings. I’ve never thought highly of Mederos and Padilla presents more danger. Now, I’m not a fan of Padilla missing weight but that still wouldn’t stop him here. If Mederos gets cut off from being able to movement like he wants to, Padilla will basically trap him and start laying in the punishment. He’ll overwhelm Mederos and get a 1st round stoppage to help people forget about losing to the scale.

Tatiana Suarez vs. Lupita Godinez
Tatiana Suarez via Decision
Another fight I hope isn’t a snooze fest. Pretty much it’s whoever’s grappling with cancel the others out and I see Suarez preventing the takedowns and getting the vast majority of the control time. This is where the wall is for Loopy where she’ll probably not skilled enough to crack the top echelon of contenders but she could still give Tatiana some problems if she’s not careful. It just won’t be enough as Suarez takes the decision win.

Mateusz Gamrot vs. Esteban Ribovics
Esteban Ribovics via Decision
Going with my gut on this one. If Gamrot gets his takedown attempts stifled, he’s in for a world of trouble. Ribovics can tune him up on the feet where he’s most vulnerable and that’s exactly what I see happening. He could even finish him but Gamrot will show heart and toughness to make it to the bell but drops a decision to Ribovics.

Kevin Holland vs. Randy Brown
Randy Brown via Decision
Nope, can’t do it. Can’t pick Kevin Holland regardless if it’s going against one of my own Cardinal Rules about recent knockouts. You never know if the dudes just there to collect his check or actually fight. Randy really wants to wipe away the memory of his most recent fight so expect him to be the aggressor and push Holland enough to get the advantage in the exchanges. Rudeboy gets the job done as it goes the distance.

Patricio Pitbull vs. Aaron Pico
Aaron Pico via Decision
Bellator never dies. I bet the matchmakers had a good laugh when they booked this one. Pitbull still has some fight left in him but he’s not going to be a threat a this point for UFC gold. With Pico, I think we all agree he can be one of the best fighters at his weight class in the world…until he decides to fight stupidly and gets knocked out because of it. At some point you’d think you’d learn your lesson but I get a spinning elbow to the face will remind you it’s different in the UFC. He’s more skilled and deadly than Pitbull and will give him the business but if he’s going to make waves here, he has to fight smarter from now on.

Cub Swanson vs. Nate Landwehr
Cub Swanson via 3rd Round TKO
This is the proper way to send Cub off into the sunset. This is the all out war he wanted with an opponent more than willing to oblige to that. These two will do nothing short of try to tear each other apart for 15 minutes and expect Cub to leave it all in the cage. He’s never been one to play it safe for a win so even if he’s ahead in the 3rd, he’s not going to slow down. He’s a fan favorite and will get his flowers on the way out with a memorable 3rd round knockout to cap his career off in style.

Dominick Reyes vs. Johnny Walker
Dominick Reyes via 1st Round TKO
I have so many reasons to not like this fight but also realize we could have a hilarious fight on our hands. Both of these dudes are the definition of a glass cannon; spots of greatness among the mundane. So it’s a take your pick situation; who’s chin breaks first? I’ll say the guy who’s been knocked out 6 times previously will make it 7 here. I’m happy Johnny has a family now and seems to be doing great outside of the cage but it might be time to be a family man. Once you lose your facilities, you’re just a prisoner in your own head and that’s no way to live when you have kids. Reyes will finish this within the first 5 minutes.

Curtis Blaydes vs. Josh Hokit
Josh Hokit via 2nd Round TKO
Oh yea, it’s happening folks. He’s a total toolbox that’s probably somewhere on the spectrum but he’s a meathead who’s fearless and can grind fighter down. A younger Blaydes beats the breaks off Hokit, but this Blaydes isn’t very intimidating and I think he just doesn’t want it anymore. Losing this one will be insult (and multiple) to injury but thems the breaks. Hulk Hokit delivers the Automatic Legdrop and sends Blaydes to a very, very dark place in his career.

Azamat Murzakanov vs. Paulo Costa
Azamat Murzakanov via 1st Round KO
I’m sad now. Fun’s over. Paulo my friend you’re about to be annihilated, old country style and it won’t take long. Azamat will hurt him early like he’s never been before and that’ll be curtains. Scary man.

Jiri Prochazka vs. Carlos Ulberg
Jiri Prochazka via 3rd Round TKO
I wanted to pick Ulberg, then I decided I’d rather be right than wrong. I think Ulberg is the most technically sound fighter in the division, especially on the feet but this is Jiri. None of that matters when you’re fighting someone who you can’t mimic in training, have never seen before and has himself fought the very best. He’s just too unorthodox to get reads on and once you think you do, he changes the rhythm and you’re now playing catchup. He’ll catch Ulberg with an uppercut and finish him with ground strikes in the 3rd to snatch the Light Heavyweight strap.

posts.author: ChrisSweeney
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R3
Let’s go for that. 2X Champ Jiri is coming. Ulberg is a tough stylistic match-up. Patient technical striking, Good low kicks. But he doesn’t really KO high-level fighters. Outside Reyes who’s obviously chinny it’s not much and I don’t think I can pick someone like that against Jiri. Jiri is gonna make him crumble under pressure he hasn’t seen before. Because that’s his entire style, I’m excited to see what that brings out of Ulberg who’s a patient fighter. But with a great chin, great head movement, and big shots Jiri is gonna get in Ulberg’s face and Ulberg’s gonna break after a good 2 rounds. I can already see the whole Jiri in the hospital with his daughter and belt thing.

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, DEC
I do think Azamat is overrated, and the more I look into the fight I think Costa has a good chance. But this is just a weird fight. I think Azamat will have the moments in the first two rounds which will win him a competitive but clear decision. I’m not worried about Azamat slowing down because Costa does too. Costa’s bodykicks will be huge this fight but who knows which version of him will show up especially after the odd move-up after a good performance. Azamat’s just so smooth he floats around and hits so hard. I think he’s gonna density-maxx and hurt Costa before they both slow down a bit. 30-27, 29-28 x2 Murzakanov.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, DEC
Too much too soon for Hokit. There’s no actual reasoning for Hokit to win this fight. I don’t think he’s gonna come out and just smoke Blaydes in the first. That’s the only way he can win is by chinning him early. And Blaydes while even being in heavyweight is a serious fighter who has skills. If it was any other blob in the top 15 Hokit would genuinely be the pick. But Curtis is too experienced. He’s the better striker outside of speed and is the more proven wrestler even if he hasn’t been using it much. Curtis doesn’t seem to be falling for his weird gimmick either, there’s just too many questions about Hokit for me to pick him against a real fighter. 3x 29-28.

Reyes vs. Walker
Dominick Reyes, TKO, R1
Obviously I have no idea how this is gonna go. But I'm gonna take the better fighter in my opinion. These dudes were on different paths before their last fights. Reyes was hoping to have this career resurgence and was fighting a real contender like Ulberg. And Johnny was supposed to get fed to the new Chinese prospect. Ended up going more Johnny’s way. Of course this fight though is just whoever finds the chin first. Johnny might have more crazy meme moves in his toolbag but I just think Reyes is the better and more technical fighter that finds the chin first. Might switch.

Swanson vs. Landwehr
Cub Swanson, DEC
Great old school scrap for Cub’s retirement, and I think he comes out on top. Cub still has a chin and cardio, which already gives him an advantage over Nate. Nate’s old himself and not on the best run getting finished twice. And I think his style plays into Cub’s fight pretty well. He loves to brawl, He looks slow and can’t take damage anymore either. All his wins are comebacks from getting beat on. Cub’s not gonna break, not gonna get KO’d and I think he will have plenty of opportunities in the pocket Nate does get hit. Hoping Cub retires with a win I’m a huge fan of his team. 3x 29-28.

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R2
Aaron Pico -300? I wanna be the contrarian and pick Pitbull so bad. Where is proof that Pico is a UFC ranked level fighter to justify the line. because there isn’t any but I just think Pitbull is gonna get speed mogged and he can get chinned as well, even though Pitbull can sit down on a punch and end it at any time because of Pico’s chin. Against Yair’s speed he barely landed and Pico is gonna put it on him he’s kill or be killed, he's either finishing the fight or getting finished. I do think Pico is good and his pressure would put away any low level guy early. He didn't look bad against Lerone for as long as he lasted. But the Ige win for Pitbull is lowk legit and is he too experienced to fall to Pico’s pressure? Pitbull does resort to wrestling a lot which I don’t think he can do as easily against Pico too. Hope this Bellator title fight hits, so small of a sample size of these dudes in the UFC. Might switch.

Holland vs. Brown
Randy Brown, DEC
The two most inconsistent fighters in the world. And the fade Kevin Holland trend continues. Can’t pick the whole “I don’t care, I’m just having fun.” talk. I’m gonna take the more serious fighter everytime. I think Holland actually has the best chance to end the fight. But I don’t think he’s gonna grapple as much as he should, and who knows how he looks after his recent performances. Brown does have a weak chin and suspicious ground game. I just think he’s better on the feet and will control the distance. Brown’s not gonna brawl this will probably be a boring fight at range and Brown picks away. Might switch. 3x 29-28.

Gamrot vs. Ribovics
Mateusz Gamrot, DEC
This is an easy pick. How many times did Radzhabov take Ribovics down? People were picking Gamrot to beat Oliveira (Not me) and all because he lost on short notice doesn’t mean he’s declined to an unranked level. To win this fight Ribovics would have to KO Mateusz, and he’s just not gonna land 250 strikes to do that without getting taken down. Ribovics is entertaining but he’s not that good and his TDD is nothing special and he doesn’t have real power. I would be shocked if Gamrot doesn’t put on a clinic and smother him. 3x 30-27 Gamrot, LW gatekeepers are the best in the UFC.

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
Tatiana is gonna lay on her, control Loopy and win. Even with Suarez coming off a bad performance.  I think Tatiana can control Loopy for at least two out of 3 rounds. Loopy doesn’t have power like Lemos or the skill on the feet. Tatiana might even control her in the third round as well. Loopy has bad cardio herself and doesn’t have good defensive wrestling. 3x 29-28.

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, SUB, R2
I have become a fan of Padilla’s game throughout his evolution in the UFC. He’s not this crazy guy but follows a good gameplan everytime. He’s smart and knows when he needs to turn it up. Stays very safe with his guard and picks his shots. Mederos is gonna throw kicks and feints, but Padilla has way more finishing/damage potential, and Padilla knows how to check a leg kick. He’s also the better striker with a reach advantage. Medero’s grappling is also a hole I think Padilla can take advantage of because Mederos gets outgrappled. I just think Medero’s mid overall and a Chris Padilla gameplan can work here on all levels.

Gastelum vs. Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, DEC
Both are washed. But I think Luque is DONE. He looks checked out, he seriously looked like he didn't wanna be in there against Alverez. Luque isn't just the same since getting that brain injury against Geoff Neal. I just don’t see a way he can win this fight at all. At least Gastelum has the chin and power, is still in the game and is competitive. Gastelum hurts him and lays on top for 2 rounds. 3x 30-27.

Radtke vs. Prado
Charles Radtke, DEC
Good fight, Low level slug fest. These dudes are gonna scrap. Prado shouldn’t be in the UFC and is gonna get hit 10,000 times, Dude takes crazy amounts of damage in every fight and can’t stop it. Radtke is a decent striker. He is a boxer who uses hooks mostly with good power and pressure. If it gets to the ground Prado is just as bad there. Radtke is not anything impressive but he can beat a non-UFC fighter who’s 1-4 and got beat on by Zellhuber. Radtke 3x 30-27. Doesn’t get a finish this time. Prado gets cut.

* Edited at 04.11.2026,  2:10 AM ET *

posts.author: finncampbell
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 2

Jiří Procházka vs. Carlos Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO R2
Ulberg is nowhere as technical has people would have you believe, he is patient and waits for his opponent to come to him, always greeting them with a lead hook and exiting to the left, that is his game. Great at managing distance, baiting his opponent into the dead space, throwing that right lead hook and exiting. Rinse and repeat. If Jiri has studied him at all, he knows this. And I think he does. From what Jiri has been saying gin the lead up, he knows what he is getting into here. Saying Carlos does not do well when pressured, which is true. He does not know what to do with his back against the cage, he has been allowed to circle out by all his opponents, like Diego chasing Volk. The 205ers Carlos has fought do not have the intelligence, or ability to cut off the cage, or if they do in the case of Jan, they don't have the athleticism to keep up with Carlos circling around him. Jiri does. With Carlos always exiting left after his lead hook, Jiri can catch him with a right hook as he cycles into it. While yes, Jiri can, and very likely will walk onto shots from Ulberg, Jiri's chin is biblical. Poatan the only guy able to put him out. The longer this one goes, the heavier Carlos' legs will be, the harder it will be to execute his fight style of constantly circling, especially so heavy at 205, he won't be able to do that for 25 mins. Carlos may outland Jiri in the first, but with Jiri saying he needs to be more aggressive early (which I don't know how he takes up another notch) I think he knows Ulbegr's best chance at victory is early, so if Jiri is the one dictating the fight from the break, Carlos will have never had such a presence before him, this isn't Alonzo Menifield. With Jiri pressing uber hard early, I can see Ulberg biting on the feints, freezing him, waiting for an opening, but with so much to read, so much pressure, so large a threat, it'll freeze him into not throwing, much like Merab v Yan I. Focussing on not getting caught as he circles and backs up. Going into the 2nd, Eugene Bareman will be demanding more volume from Ulberg, but with Ulberg's very limited arsenal he only really has that lead hook to go to, which Jiri and his coaches will be very aware of. All that to say, I am very confident Jiri wins. Jiri knocking Ulberg out to regain Light Heavyweight gold in late round 2, or round 3. Prochazka Rd2 TKO. 75% Confidence.

Azamat Murzakanov vs. Paulo Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, DEC
Costa would be a play if I could trust him to be motivated to win and follow a gameplan, originally picking him but him saying he plans on going back down to 185 tells me he isn't taking ts seriously anymore. Azamat's power is gonna put the fear of god in him. After Azamat's opponents feel the power, the look in their eye is different and Costa is a willing quitter; eg. the Adesanya fight where he just looked like he didn't want to be there and folded on a glancing blow, balled up and quit, and the Strickland fight where the pace and pressure had him resigned to defeat within the first two rounds. Once Costa gets hit cleanly once, he won't want anymore. Keeping distance, low volume kicks, enough to not get hurt but not doing enough damage, low teens at best for significant strikes per round in the first two. He could win a decision outworking Azamat in the 2nd and 3rd but I don't see him coming out of his shell until the 3rd. There could be an argument for lighter man skill here, but Azamat is so fast and Costa will have never felt power like this at 185. 29-28 Murzakanov. 55% Confidence.

Curtis Blaydes vs. Josh Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, TKO R2
Hokit has not proven himself against this level, or even a few rungs below this level. It is a huge step up here against an experienced, battle proven vet. A large size disparity too, Hokit to win has to press early for the finish, he is not winning a decision here, he won't get Blaydes down, his best chance is an early finish. Which is possible with his hand speed, but if he can't put Denzel Freeman out, I can't see him doing it to Blaydes. He is no Aspinall, he isn't Pavlovich, he isn't Derrick Lewis. People picking against Blaydes are citing his last fight with Kuniev, where he looked stuck in the mud and sluggish, I strongly believe Blaydes when he says his knee was cooked in the first. The length and size of Blaydes is a huge factor for me, while the speed and pressure of Hokit definitely aids him in the first, if he doesn't get the finish there, it is all Blaydes. Blaydes will win the 2nd & 3rd by landing more meaningful shots and mixing in takedowns. Hokit is really going to struggle to get Blaydes down when/if he can't get to Blaydes' head. With Hokit's aggressive first round, they are going to trade in the pocket, the speed is the biggest asset Hokit has, so if he gets to the target first, he can finish Blaydes, butt again, i have to favour the experience, proven ability, size and length of Blaydes to land. I see Hokit winning the 1st, but not dropping Blaydes, winning it his his pace and pressure but gassing early in the second. Blaydes getting him down as Hokit slows, tiring as his volume slumps, Blaydes elbowing his little head in with G&D. Blaydes Rd2 TKO. 55% Confidence.

Dominick Reyes vs. Johnny Walker
Johnny Walker, TKO R1
Anything could happen. I am siding with chaos and unpredictability with Walker landing cleanly before Reyes does, a spinning backfist/elbow that deletes Reyes.

Cub Swanson vs. Nate Landwehr
Cub Swanson, TKO R2
Both guys will come in here wanting to put on a show, likely the fight of the night (if it wasn't a Jiri card), Nate is very hittable, very very hittable and Cub is a high-level boxer. While Cub is coming off a year hiatus, at his age with his experience I am not worried about ring-rust, he may open slow, catching reads and feeling himself into the fight, much like Nate does, but once this fight gets going is going to be fun. Cub is 4-1 in his last (he beat Fili), so any argument against his age I do not care for, Nate has shown nothing against this level of fighter in a long time, his wins have against guys at the entry level of the UFC, building his resume on baby Onama and baby Klein, who have now developed beyond him. Both guys will come into the pocket and trade, they both it's there last big showing, last big fight on a PPV main card and they both love putting on a show. Cub is just the better boxer, andante he realises it, extending and combinations and pressing Nate like Charriere did, he will crumble Nate. Swanson Rd2 TKO. 65% Confidence.

Patricio Pitbull vs. Aaron Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO R1
Pico has been saying all the right stuff in the lead up to this, but his history tells me different. I think he presses hard and aggressive early, looking to find the finish. Dana loves himself a good little American white boy who puts on fire fights, regardless if they win or lose. A professional company man he can promote as the good American baby face (eg. Michael Chandler) and Pico gives me that vibe. He wants to be beloved, he wants the highlights, he wants the attention. So, looking aggressively for a finish comes naturally, he has a lot of power, an elite athlete with a strong wrestling base, sound familiar? But Pitbull is no bum, while he is no longer the fighter he used to be, or likely never been top 5 level at featherweight, but he is experienced, experienced and wily enough to know what's coming. It's gonna be a fire fight for as long as it goes, Pitbull tucks his chin, waiting for his open, which there will be many. If Pico fights the way I expect him to, there will be a finish, either with Pico crashing in, Pitbull covering up, dropping to his knees or getting dropped and G&P'd to a finish or Pitbull finding his open opening as Pico comes in, putting him down very similar to Lerone, out hard and cold. The u2.5, or even the u1.5, feels like a great play. The only reason I am picking Pico is the market's confidence in him, but with the price they are offering on Pitbull having something on him is a good play too. I may switch just before the card starts, the closer I have gotten to this fight the more I am leaning Pitbull. Pico Rd1 TKO. 51% Confidence.

Kevin Holland vs. Randy Brown
Kevin Holland, TKO R2
Ugh. I love this matchmaking (which is a rare thing to say in 26') because I have no clue. Making the argument for Randy is so much easier, he should win, he should be the pick. He takes his career seriously, he wants it more, he will likely fight by a gameplan and Holland has shown nothing particularly impressive since 2020. His wins since then; Alex Oliveira, Tim Means, Santiago Ponzinibbio, Michael Chiesa, Michal Oleksiejczuk, Gunnar Nelson & Vicente Luque, all of which, barring Michal, are retired or should be retired. Any fight with reasonable resistance, he loses. And there will be resistance here. So, my brain is telling me to pick Randy. But, I don't know. I don't trust Randy's style here, despite his height and reach he is not good at managing distance, he actually likes to come in himself and fire, which I love here for Holland, who is the longer fighter. I don't trust Randy's chin and Holland has power, I don't see any world where Randy finishes Holland. I think Randy wins this until he doesn't. Randy has been finished 5 times, 4 of which were in the 2nd round, I can see Randy winning the first with Holland finding that chin the 2nd, either finishing him there, or following it up with a quick SUB. Holland Rd2. 50% Confidence.

Mateusz Gamrot vs. Esteban Ribovics
Mateusz Gamrot, DEC
Ribovics has not proven himself near this level, this is the exact type of fighter Gamrot beats. A kickboxer with provenly bad TD defence, even if he buffs the first attempt, he will get him down every round and the longer the fight goes the worse it's going to get. Wins like Klein, Fisiev, Turner and Ferreira should've proven that by now, but seeing so many people Ribovis has me perplexed, what are they seeing I'm not? Ribovics' resume is not aging well, he does not have KO power like people thought he did, he isn't Hooker, he isn't Dariush, and he certainly isn't Do Bronx. He MUST finish Gamrot to win, and Ribovics is not getting a sub here, he needs a KO against a guy who has never been KO'd. He will need to pressure Gamrot and get off extended combinations with some regularity to win, but again, I don't think he has the power required to put Gamrot out, he may drop him early, but if he can't finish Zellhuber with 156 significant strikes, I can't see him doing it tired against the best fight the has ever fought. 30-27 Gamrot. 70% Confidence.

Tatiana Suarez vs. Loopy Godinez
Loopy Godinez, DEC
Very possible upset here, Tatiana has not looked as dominant since coming off those injuries that kept her out for 3 years, despite that the narrative among the media and MMA fans is she's uber dominant, practically the same fighter she was 6-7 years ago. This was apparent in her fight with Weili who schooled her everywhere, embarrassingly including the grappling. But perhaps the Lemos fight was an even worse look, shooting 3-12 on takedowns against a smaller, slimmer girl who has a 63% TD defence was startling. Once she she got her down, she kept her there but the fact is was that difficult should send off alarms. Loopy's smaller stature at 5'2", making for a stockier build at 115 helps her here with the lower centre of gravity. Loopy has a 81% TD defence rate, which is elite for women's MMA, now, against prime Tatiana I wouldn't take that into consideration as much with her TD ability being so far beyond any other woman at 115, but today, Tatiana is really gonna struggle to get this to the ground. Meaning this is likely going to be on the feet for large portions of this fight, where Tatiana looks like her fiancee, Patchy Mix; stiff, slow, rigid with no head movement. Loopy is the far better boxer and will win the contest on the feet without a doubt, and if there's a concern whether Tatiana can get it down, Loopy looks a great dog here. (Initially writing this I had picked Suarez but as I was typing I realised everything I was saying favoured Loopy). 29-28 Godinez. 52% Confidence.

MarQuel Mederos vs. Chris Padilla
Chris Padilla, DEC
I do not know enough about MarQuel tbh, so naturally siding with Padilla. Taco is an aggressive, front-foot fighter, against a distance kickboxer like Mederos, who wants to leg kick and post at distance, Taco will find a way. He has the longer reach which will help him get inside and do damage while Mederos disengages and rotates around the cage, plus, the ground game massively favours Padilla here. 29-28 Padilla. 52% Confidence.

Kelvin Gastelum vs. Vicente Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, TKO R2
Luque is more washed, Kelvin is not who he was in 2018-19, but he should be more than enough for brain-bleed Luque. Likely on the feet for as long as it lasts, Kelvin hasn't fallen off the way Luque has, the Chris Curtis, D-Rod and Pyfer verify that. He has one of the best chins in UFC history (honestly top 3). Open stance, I see Kelvin lining up that left straight down the middle and putting Luque down violently, maybe not out cold, but enough to not warrant a follow-up. Gastelum Rd2 TKO. 60% Confidence. (I am beginning to worry we all have this wrong and Luque gets an early SUB, like an anaconda or d'arce, making it look easy; a lot like the Gorimbo win.)

Charles Radtke vs. Francisco Prado
Charles Radtke, SUB R2
Charles should be larger favourite here, heavy handed wrestler against Prado, who, in all respects is not a 170 pounder, naturally should be fighting at 165, much like a Colby or a Kevin Holland, but at a much lower level. At 5'9" and a short wingspan, he needs to get in the pocket, which Radtke may welcome, in which I favour Radtke's power and technique. I trust Prado's chin though, so if it is a stand-up fight it is likely to go the 15. The large divider is the wrestling, Prado is bad at it. Very bad. If Radtke feels like he is losing on the feet, at any time he can shoot and likely get it down. I see a similar performance to the Blood Diamond and Daniel Frunza fights, where he gets a lot of control time. I can see Prado doing well in the 1st, which only encourages the wrestling out of Radtke in the 2nd, which he will get, eventually finding an arm-triangle or RNC. Radtke Rd2 SUB. 70% Confidence.

posts.author: Xion
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, DEC
OIOIOIOIOI this fight is fun as ****. Murzakanov by midgetmaxxing masterclass. Costa is still pretty ******* nasty and has the power and speed but Azamat is so technically sound and has a way bigger speed advantage over Costa than any of his past opponents. The power as well from Azamat is ridiculous and I think he’s gonna get a really clean knockdown at some point but Costa will fight through and make it past R3. U/D for Murzakanov. Clean performance but Costa yet again doesn’t lose much stock considering who he was facing and because he looked pretty decent like in his other losses.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, TKO, R2
Hokits goofy cringe ass is getting pounded out. Don’t get me wrong I like that someone’s making an effort to actually make the UFC interesting for a change but still it’s quite obnoxious and sometimes a bit gay. Anyway Curtis Blaydes is still top **** at HW and he was so cooked during the Kuniev fight so don’t even bring that up. Guy had one leg for the whole fight. Josh Hokit is actually very good too and he’s definitely making people underestimate him because of his antics and that’s not good because he is very good for fat UFC heavyweight standards. I still think Blaydes is gonna win but Hokit will look pretty good and be shocking a lot of people with his skill no doubt (again, for a fat heavyweight it’s impressive). I’ve got Blaydes by G&P TKO or close U/D. Good fight and there will be a lot of wrestling scrambles but Blaydes will have the strength advantage I reckon.

Reyes vs. Walker
Dominick Reyes, TKO, R1
A3 vs A4. The battle of the paper chins. Reyes takes this or at least he should. Johnny has looked better recently against Zhang Mingyang but he still leaves his guard out like a zombie when he gets in range against his opponents and Reyes is a ******* insane counter striker. Guys got a ******* supersonic piston for a left hand. He’ll catch Walker coming in with some wild **** and put him to sleep.

Swanson vs. Landwehr
Cub Swanson, DEC
FOTN automatically. Give them both 100k js for show money atp. ******* BANGER! I can’t wait. I think Cub Swanson should win this fight but Nate Landwehr is a bizarre beast. He’ll lose in the goofiest way to the goofiest opponent but then look goated in other fights. Cub has definitely still got it at his age which is crazy to me and he’s just a beast of a fighter in general and I think he’ll be rocking Nate’s **** left and right during this fight and he’ll take this win pretty clearly but Landwehr is gonna make it tough as ****. He’ll be throwing wild every chance he gets and it’s gonna be so fun to watch. Sad to see Cub go ******* legend bro.

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R2
Aaron Pico hype train is back on the tracks gang. WE will show the world who was wrong about us. I think he’s genuinely in the top 5 FW conversation rn and if it wasn’t for his occasional poor defence and dog**** chin he’d be a champ but he just gets caught sometimes and it stops his momentum and it sucks for his career. But I think he can get this one and look good doing it. Pitbull is very good and he showed that against Dan Ige but Ige is on a decline as of late so I can’t really put too much stock into that fight and Pico has such good boxing and power I can’t just see him controlling the fight and beating up Pitbull who can sometimes easily get pressured and hit. I’ll say Pico KO’s him with a flurry of nasty punishment after rocking his **** midway through round 2.

Holland vs. Brown
Randy Brown, DEC
I like Randy Brown beating up Holland here. Cant trust this guy to do anything anymore. Idk if he even knows what a 45 70 is anymore🫩. Randy Brown has always shown he id really decent and I think Kevin Holland is actually really **** lowkey I just don’t trust him to out do someone as active on the feet as Randy Brown over 15.

Gamrot vs. Ribovics
Mateusz Gamrot, DEC
Mateusz Gamrot is gonna win but it’s gonna be a dirty and gritty fight and Ribovics is gonna look very good. Wouldn’t be surprised if he takes it off damage but I think Gamrot is still top 3 best 155 grapplers rn and he’ll show that once again. Relentless pressure and will actually engage with Ribovics and just mix in takedowns and frustrate him to get a split decision win. Close competitive fight but Gamer gets the nod🇵🇱

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
Definitely Suarez is the better fighter in this matchup. Not much to say here. “The Female Khabib🤓”

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, TKO, R3
I think Chris Padilla is a beast and will show us why MarQuel Mederos isn’t all that yet. He’s gonna get outclassed everywhere and I honestly think we gotta push Padilla towards the rankings soon he’s been making pretty clean work of these good fighters outside the rankings. I reckon he will wear Mederos down and finish him late with an against the fence TKO.

Gastelum vs. Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, DEC
Gastelum prolly gets a relatively easy win imo. Luque is way past it and as much as it pains me to say as a Vicente Luque fan he just can’t go up against this level of comp anymore even though I think he’s one of the better washed up fighters in the ufc rn. I just think in this point of his career Gastelum is better and faster in the striking and can land the more impactful shots over 3 and keep Luque guessing. Imma say Fridge Kelvin by 29-28 U/D.

Radtke vs. Prado
Charles Radtke, DEC
I think Radtke can outlast Prado over 3 rounds and I really don’t rate the Veretennikov fight at all and Radtke is pretty good so I think he gets this one.

posts.author: UncleSocrates
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Can someone who is strong on the Hokit side explain to me if I am missing something? Hokit has had 2 ufc fights against two bottom of the barrel heavyweights and now is taking a ginormous step up to fight Blaydes. Even at this stage of his career Blaydes is far and beyond better than anyone Hokit has faced. Hokit looked very good in his two fights in the ufc but Gimenis is a bjj nerd that even found success in the leg kicks and Freeman legitimately does nothing in the cage. Hokit’s wrestling has seen no resistance and his hands are fast for heavyweight standards but there are so many unknowns. Blaydes is a very good striker for hw standards and has the wrestling acumen to defend Hokit’s takedowns or at the least get back to his feet, I would be shocked if Hokit controls him for an extended period of time and if this gets extended on the feet there’s no telling if Hokit reliably can go for 3 rounds. We know Blaydes has the cardio to support an extended fight, he has fought the cream of the crop at heavyweight for years now, and even if not used offensively we know he has the wrestling foundation to handle himself defensively in that department. The only clear edge I see for Hokit is that Blaydes doesn’t react to getting hit well and can be hurt. But do we even know how Hokit reacts to getting hit? Does he have a chin? How does he react when his takedown gets stuffed in the third round and he is stuck on the feet?

For me personally, there is way too many unknowns to justify a pickem price which is what is confusing me, especially given we only know for sure of one reliable path to winning which is him blitzing and chinning Blaydes early. If these guys fight 20 times does that outcome happen enough to justify Hokit being a slight favorite? Please help me understand bc this is a head scratcher for me rn

Prediction:

Procházka vs. Ulberg

posts.author: Spockling
TheAnalyzer

TheAnalyzer
Xion
posts.content:
Prediction:
Procházka  |  KO/TKO  |  Round 3

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R3
Damn this is such a banger fight for the title. I’ve still got Jiri winning. He’s just too ******* high level and autistic to lose to anyone but Alex Pereira and he’s literally the most ferociously consistent finisher on the roster. Carlos Ulberg is very good though don’t get me wrong and he definitely has the power and skill to back it up but I’ve seen him struggle with old man Jan and get in a close dogfight with Oezdemir. Jiri out dogged and slept Oezdemir in about 6 minutes and the dude wasn’t even in his prime yet. Don’t get me wrong styles make fights and I’m not basing this prediction off who did better against these opponents but there are a lot of tells in these matchups and in their specific styles that show me Ulberg isn’t ready to deal with someone like Jiri and I’ll give you a simple explanation as to why. Willpower. Nobody is built the same as Jiri Prochazka and that’s why nobody’s gonna beat him. He just doesn’t ******* lose. Doesn’t matter how **** he looks. He’ll come back into the fight and just flip the switch and hunt you down. I’m gonna keep going with the trend of Jiri’s 3rd round KO wins and say he gets it done around there. He’s gonna break Ulberg with pressure and unrelenting output. He’ll gas him out and hurt him on multiple occasions. I see this playing out very similarly to the Rakic fight but Ulberg looks better, moves better, and lasts longer in there than Rakic, but the result is the same. As with ALL Jiri’s opponents (bar Pereira). Ulberg is a very good counter striker though I’ll give him his credit where it’s due. He has extremely powerful hands and he knows where to place them and when and he does has a very similar style to Izzy in the pocket which is very on trend for the guys a CKB. When they get backed up they kinda just lean back and spam hooks and pray they don’t get cracked in the process. Ulberg has a sniper of a left hook though and he’s really good at backing up and walking guys into it and Jiri is the type to just come bumbling forward into punches but he just has the most granite chin of all time and even if he does get hurt, THE Samurai Holy Ghost is literally protecting this man. I’m sorry but unless you are a prehistoric hunter gatherer from an ancient tribe of Brazilians with stones for hands you cannot defeat this man in battle. It’s just not possible. And if Carlos Ulberg wins the gods let it be so that Jiri can ascend. But he has already ascended higher than anyone at 205 can reach. And he’ll stay there. At the top WITH THE BELT. LETS GO FOR THAT//\\🇨🇿⚡️⚔️⚡️🇨🇿

This might be a hot take, but strikers should not lose to grapplers unless the guy is a top 5 grappler in the division or unless the guy has pretty good hands to complement his grappling. posts.content:

Ulberg  |  Decision

I can't believe so many people picked Ribovics, the guy is a punching bag and just spams hooks mindlessly, no grappling at all other than being gigantic (weight bully walking around 190-200 lbs) and being able to win many scrambles thanks to that size and mass advantage. How can he beat Gamrot? maybe throw 300 strikes hoping for one of them hurts Mateusz hitting a lottery ticket?

I know this will get at least 5 or 10 dislikes cause Ribovics has a lot of simps that hype him up for no real reason, the guy is absurdly untalented. Goodt athlete, great guy, tough fighter, and also a very humble and nice dude; i'm a fan of his, nothing against him... it's just the black-and-white truth, he's not even mid and he should not be fighting close to the rankings (argentinian privilege is crazy, getting a top-10 opponent after 3 mediocre wins, just like his compatriot Kevin Vallejos did last month)

don't be surprised if Ulberg mixes up takedowns effectively. Jiri looks only good when he fights static opponents with no footwork and no wrestling, most of them southpaws who can not kick his calf from the outside.

If Ulberg loses, MMA makes no logical sense, and the technique and level of skill very well be completely irrelevant; may well just decide the results and picks based on who has more power of will,  even if it's a sloppy mess that blocks strikes with his face like Jiri, and why even do any research or watch any footage to analyze a fight.

I love Jiri, but he has no logical path to victory. I can't pick him based on my love for him and just hoping and praying on the hypothetical instance of Ulberg breaking somehow in the later rounds

posts.content:
Pico always look great when's taking over but i still can't trust him. He has shown better defense and awareness of it in this fight but you can see pitbull is fresh and fast in 1st round, he's able to get into pico's range and hit him despite being much shorter. And his reaction to those shots weren't great.

I think Pico success on 145 depends on the the styles matchup. I would favor him against Costa or maybe Yair. But guys like Vallejos, Silva, Lopes, and Allen would most likely finish him.

People focused too much on that previous loss to Vettori just because it was at 205. It wasn't relevant. That fight was only at 205 because Costa was hurt and didn't come close to making weight. The only reason the fight happened was because it was a main event and Vettori agreed to the weight class change. But it was a terrible fight and probably should have been canceled.

This was different because Paulo chose this fight and it was supposed to be at 205. First finish in like 8 years. Great performance.
